title: indie Semiconductor Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results
text: indie Semiconductor reported Q2 2025 revenue of $53.4 million, down 4.5% sequentially from Q1 2025. The company posted a non-GAAP gross profit of $26.7 million with a 50% gross margin. Non-GAAP operating loss was $11.5 million compared to $10.9 million in Q2 2024. Net loss per share on a GAAP basis was $0.13, while non-GAAP loss per share was $0.05. The company faced macroeconomic headwinds including tariff uncertainty and inventory destocking across the automotive supply chain. Management noted that despite near-term challenges, long-term demand drivers for ADAS, in-cabin, and electrification solutions remain strong. For Q3 2025, the company guides revenue of $52-56 million with the midpoint of $54 million. The company ended Q2 with $189.2 million in cash and cash equivalents. Management highlighted continued progress on multiple design wins set to launch in late 2025 and 2026, including radar for autonomous mobility and vision processors for humanoid robotics.
